scenario: Round 2 Islamabad paused (Apr 25); Araghchi Oman back-channel (Sultan Haitham, Apr 26) reopens narrow diplomatic window; returns Islamabad; next Moscow; Trump 'shoot and kill' ROE for IRGC minelaying boats (Apr 23); Brent ~$106 est. Apr 27; blockade intact; Iran holds 3 Western vessels; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; tail risk 47%

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat edges higher on Brent +0.6% (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel (Apr 26) gives fractional reprieve - tail-risk premium eases to 47%. P&I cancellation, blockade, and Cape routing cost premium remain intact. Fertilizer supply disruption mechanism unchanged. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation follows Brent ~$106 est. (Apr 27). Oman back-channel (Apr 26) slightly reduces tail risk to 47%; fertilizer and freight disruption mechanism unchanged - blockade intact.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs follow Brent ~$106 est. (Apr 27). Oman back-channel gives fractional relief; tail 47%. Cape routing costs elevated; structural supply chain disruption unresolved - blockade and P&I cancellation intact.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO follows Brent ~$106 est. (Apr 27). Oman back-channel mildly positive; tail 47%. Indonesia B40 mandate provides structural floor. Malaysia energy emergency (June supply uncertain, RON95 quota cut) adds domestic cost pressure. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting cycle unresolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure eases fractionally on Oman back-channel (Apr 26) - narrow diplomatic window reopened; tail 47%. Q2 planting season urea availability risk persists; blockade intact; Qafco force majeure unresolved. Malaysia June supply gap risk remains.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha follows Brent ~$106 est. (Apr 27); Oman back-channel (Apr 26) gives fractional relief. Blockade remains - Gulf condensate interdiction persists; Qatar force majeure unresolved; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 (The National). Pengerang RAPID feedstock exposure elevated; tail 47%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene follows naphtha on Brent +0.6% (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel marginally positive. SABIC and QAPCO export bottlenecks persist - blockade intact; vessel interdiction not lifted; tail 47%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol edges higher with Brent (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel opens narrow window. US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure unresolved; blockade intact. China MTO complexes see incremental feedstock pressure. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea edges up (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel (Apr 26) gives fractional relief - narrow window reopened; tail 47%. Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; blockade intact. US sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP edges higher (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel (Apr 26) opens narrow window; tail 47%. Saudi Ma'aden shipping still disrupted; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade intact. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam remains acute.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs stable (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel mildly positive. Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP - freight remains main transmission. Cape rerouting costs persist; palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia face moderately elevated input costs.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI flat to marginally higher (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel (Apr 26) opens narrow diplomatic window but blockade and P&I suspension persist. Normalization still distant: mine-clearing 6 months even on deal; physical Hormuz reopening delayed regardless. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates stable at elevated levels (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel (Apr 26) marginally positive but blockade intact, P&I suspension unresolved, Hormuz not reopened. Iran holds 3 Western vessels; tail (47%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Container rates stable to marginally higher (Apr 27 est.); Oman back-channel opens narrow window but Cape rerouting premium persists - blockade intact, P&I unresolved. MSC Francesca still seized; Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration; tail 47%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (est.).
